frameworks.name: Granger causality
frameworks.one_line: statistical inference of directed influence between recorded brain signals

frameworks.name: transfer entropy
frameworks.one_line: information-theoretic measure linked to Granger causality

frameworks.name: dynamical causal modelling
frameworks.one_line: alternative causal framework mentioned for comparison

core_question: How do existing time, frequency, and time-varying Granger-causality estimators compare in neuroscience signal analysis?
key_assumptions: neural signals admit linear autoregressive representations
causality can be operationalised via predictive improvement
stationarity holds within analysis windows for spectral variants
manipulations: domain of analysis (time vs frequency vs time-varying)
estimator family (classical, partial, conditional, DTF, PDC)
key_findings: unified framework relates time-domain Granger causality to transfer entropy
spectral and time-varying extensions differ in estimation and distributional properties
many estimator variants exist with distinct assumptions and pitfalls
interpretations: methodological choice strongly conditions causal claims about brain signals
systematic comparison is needed given the proliferation of estimators
limitations_noted: partial/conditional Granger causality and DCM/DTF/PDC are mentioned but not the focus
